storage_status.historical_loss_note: Phase 11 records that the 2026-04 raw and detector-input archive tree was already deleted before this runtime revision work, so some older windows are no longer locally restorable.

retention.notes: Do not prune canonical DB state without an explicit dump or backup.

reproducibility_contract.reason: Archived-window Phase 3 replay and late detector activation recovery depend on detector-input partitions.
These tables prove which partitions existed, which are missing, and what was lost.
Persisted candidates, alerts, scores, checkpoints, and restore/audit ledgers live here.
These are frozen reference evidence packets already cited by later-phase docs.
Raw envelopes preserve source-of-truth payloads for auditability and republish validation.
Useful for incident debugging but not a long-term reproducibility requirement.

retention_policy.operator_rule: Do not delete blindly; use storage audit, compaction plan, and restore plan first.
This is the minimum archive needed for archived-window Phase 3 replay.
SQLite file or PostgreSQL dump must exist before invasive cleanup.
Safe pruning target. Older logs are helpful but not part of the replay contract.
Prune ad hoc replay output; preserve frozen evidence packets referenced by docs.
Safe pruning target because these reports can be regenerated from current state.
